# Satori

Satori is a Godot 4.6 game project written in GDScript. Use this file as the compact starting context for Codex, Claude, GitHub Copilot, and other LLM agents before loading larger specs or source trees.

## Start here

- Agent rules: `AGENTS.md`
- Claude rules: `CLAUDE.md`
- Copilot rules: `.github/agents/copilot-instructions.md`
- Current design spine: `specs/master/master_plan.md`, `specs/master/spec.md`, `specs/master/recipes.md`
- Active gameplay specs: `specs/022-ritual-menu-slots/`, `specs/023-biome-natural-materials/`, `specs/024-spirit-assistants-components/`, `specs/025-rain-kami-path/`
- Fast validation: `tools/godot.ps1`, `check-errors.ps1`

## Token-saving setup

This repository is configured for Headroom as an optional context optimization layer:

- Local MCP config for Claude/Codex-compatible MCP hosts: `.mcp.json`
- VS Code/GitHub Copilot workspace MCP config: `.vscode/mcp.json`
- Setup and run helper: `tools/headroom.ps1`
- Full operator notes: `docs/headroom-token-savings.md`

Install once per machine:

```powershell
pip install "headroom-ai[all]"
npm install headroom-ai
```

Then verify:

```powershell
.\tools\headroom.ps1 -Action Verify
```

Use the MCP tools for large file reads, search output, logs, diffs, JSON, and generated reports:

- `headroom_compress` before pasting or reasoning over large content
- `headroom_retrieve` when exact original text is needed
- `headroom_stats` to inspect savings

For automatic proxy mode:

```powershell
.\tools\headroom.ps1 -Action Proxy
```

Other Headroom CLI modes after the Python install succeeds:

```powershell
headroom wrap claude
headroom wrap codex
headroom wrap copilot
headroom perf
headroom dashboard
```

## Satori guardrails

- Prefer existing services, autoloads, resources, and UI plumbing over parallel systems.
- Do not give an autoload singleton key the same name as a script `class_name`.
- Avoid `:=` when the right-hand side can be `Variant` in warnings-as-errors files.
- Prefer `preload("res://...")` for typed cross-script dependencies.
- Keep the 2-token ritual lane reserved for mixed seeds unless the design explicitly changes.
- Preserve progression gates unless the current task asks to rebalance them.
- After script or typed-signal changes, run a parse/error check before finalizing.

## Validation

Common checks:

```powershell
.\tools\godot.ps1 -Command parse
.\tools\godot.ps1 -Command boot
.\tools\godot.ps1 -Command test -Test res://tests/unit/<test_file>.gd
```

Use focused checks for the changed path first. Treat the known shutdown `ObjectDB` leak warning separately from real failures.
